other_observers: None; As far as I know, I found the birds.
specific_location: Escalante Canyon, approximately 7 miles from Highway 50, at a point where the stream is right next to the road but bending away, and a single small box elder rises above a busted red cattle gate on the shoreline.
description: Both birds were identical. They were about the size and shape of Say's Phoebes, but they were sooty coal-black all over, except for a snow-white patch on the belly that extended about halfway up the breast to end in a sharp point. They had no wingbars or apparent markings in the tail. They had long straight bills and their heads had a vaguely peaked or crested look.Behaviors: The birds were flycatching from perches right along the streambank, never more than about 12 feet off the water. They spent almost all their time either perched or flycatching inside the airspace of the river.Call: One or both birds made a brief, high-pitched sharp chip note several times, but it was difficult to hear over the noise of the river.Plumage: Adult
